title: Xtra Savory Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A salty, molten, and comforting Algerian dish featuring dried leaves of summer savory and a miso marinade, perfect for a sick day. This kosher-friendly recipe is sure to become a favorite.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together miso paste, olive oil, dried summer savory leaves, salt, and black pepper to make the marinade.
Add the lamb pieces to the marinade and toss to coat, then cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
In a large Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat and sear the marinated lamb pieces until browned on all sides.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it is softened and translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the dried apricots, chicken broth, and browned lamb pieces to the pot, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven, where it will cook for 2.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly before serving.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
